initial_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 1
Allowed decisions: ['execute_locally']
Prompt family: execute
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:


User prompt:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

Question:
Perform a comparative analysis between Quantum Darwinism (QD) and Holographic Quantum Error Correction (HQEC). 1) Identify core principles: QD (environment-induced decoherence, redundancy of information in sub-systems, emergence of classicality) vs. HQEC (bulk-boundary correspondence, entanglement wedge reconstruction, code subspace protection). 2) Map intersections: Redundancy in QD (multiple observers accessing same info) vs. Redundancy in HQEC (local boundary operators reconstructing bulk info); Information locality vs. Error correction thresholds. 3) Identify gaps: Lack of a unified mathematical formalism linking the pointer states of QD to the logical operators of HQEC, and the absence of formal proofs connecting the rate of information proliferation in QD to the code distance in HQEC.
